speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What shall we do, Enobarbus?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Think, and die.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Is Antony or we in fault for this?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Antony only, that would make his will

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Lord of his reason. What though you fled

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: From that great face of war, whose several ranges

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Frighted each other? Why should he follow?

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: The itch of his affection should not then

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Have nicked his captainship, at such a point,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: When half to half the world opposed, he being

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: The merèd question. ’Twas a shame no less

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Than was his loss, to course your flying flags

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: And leave his navy gazing.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Prithee, peace.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Is that his answer?

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: Ay, my lord.

speaker: ANTONY
text: The Queen shall then have courtesy, so she

speaker: ANTONY
text: Will yield us up?

speaker: AMBASSADOR
text: He says so.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Let her know ’t.—

speaker: ANTONY
text: To the boy Caesar send this grizzled head,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And he will fill thy wishes to the brim

speaker: ANTONY
text: With principalities.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That head, my lord?

speaker: ANTONY
text: To him again. Tell him he wears the rose

speaker: ANTONY
text: Of youth upon him, from which the world should note

speaker: ANTONY
text: Something particular: his coin, ships, legions

speaker: ANTONY
text: May be a coward’s, whose ministers would prevail

speaker: ANTONY
text: Under the service of a child as soon

speaker: ANTONY
text: As i’ th’ command of Caesar. I dare him therefore

speaker: ANTONY
text: To lay his gay caparisons apart

speaker: ANTONY
text: And answer me declined, sword against sword,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Ourselves alone. I’ll write it. Follow me.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Yes, like enough, high-battled Caesar will

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Unstate his happiness and be staged to th’ show

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Against a sworder! I see men’s judgments are

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: A parcel of their fortunes, and things outward

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Do draw the inward quality after them

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: To suffer all alike. That he should dream,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Knowing all measures, the full Caesar will

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Answer his emptiness! Caesar, thou hast subdued

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: His judgment too.

speaker: SERVANT
text: A messenger from Caesar.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What, no more ceremony? See, my women,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Against the blown rose may they stop their nose

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That kneeled unto the buds.—Admit him, sir.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Mine honesty and I begin to square.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: The loyalty well held to fools does make

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Our faith mere folly. Yet he that can endure

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: To follow with allegiance a fall’n lord

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Does conquer him that did his master conquer,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: And earns a place i’ th’ story.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Caesar’s will?

speaker: THIDIAS
text: Hear it apart.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: None but friends. Say boldly.

speaker: THIDIAS
text: So haply are they friends to Antony.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: He needs as many, sir, as Caesar has,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Or needs not us. If Caesar please, our master

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Will leap to be his friend. For us, you know

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Whose he is we are, and that is Caesar’s.

speaker: THIDIAS
text: So.—

speaker: THIDIAS
text: Thus then, thou most renowned: Caesar entreats

speaker: THIDIAS
text: Not to consider in what case thou stand’st

speaker: THIDIAS
text: Further than he is Caesar.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Go on; right royal.

speaker: THIDIAS
text: He knows that you embrace not Antony

speaker: THIDIAS
text: As you did love, but as you feared him.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O!

speaker: THIDIAS
text: The scars upon your honor therefore he

speaker: THIDIAS
text: Does pity as constrainèd blemishes,

speaker: THIDIAS
text: Not as deserved.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: He is a god and knows

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What is most right. Mine honor was not yielded,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: But conquered merely.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: To be sure of that,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: I will ask Antony. Sir, sir, thou art so leaky

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: That we must leave thee to thy sinking, for

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Thy dearest quit thee.

speaker: THIDIAS
text: Shall I say to Caesar

speaker: THIDIAS
text: What you require of him? For he partly begs

speaker: THIDIAS
text: To be desired to give. It much would please him

speaker: THIDIAS
text: That of his fortunes you should make a staff

speaker: THIDIAS
text: To lean upon. But it would warm his spirits

speaker: THIDIAS
text: To hear from me you had left Antony

speaker: THIDIAS
text: And put yourself under his shroud,

speaker: THIDIAS
text: The universal landlord.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: What’s your name?

speaker: THIDIAS
text: My name is Thidias.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Most kind messenger,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Say to great Caesar this in deputation:

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I kiss his conqu’ring hand. Tell him I am prompt

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: To lay my crown at ’s feet, and there to kneel.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Tell him, from his all-obeying breath I hear

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: The doom of Egypt.

speaker: THIDIAS
text: ’Tis your noblest course.

speaker: THIDIAS
text: Wisdom and fortune combating together,

speaker: THIDIAS
text: If that the former dare but what it can,

speaker: THIDIAS
text: No chance may shake it. Give me grace to lay

speaker: THIDIAS
text: My duty on your hand.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Your Caesar’s father oft,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: When he hath mused of taking kingdoms in,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Bestowed his lips on that unworthy place

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: As it rained kisses.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Favors? By Jove that thunders!

speaker: ANTONY
text: What art thou, fellow?

speaker: THIDIAS
text: One that but performs

speaker: THIDIAS
text: The bidding of the fullest man and worthiest

speaker: THIDIAS
text: To have command obeyed.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: You will be whipped.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Approach there!—Ah, you kite!—Now, gods and devils,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Authority melts from me. Of late when I cried “Ho!”

speaker: ANTONY
text: Like boys unto a muss kings would start forth

speaker: ANTONY
text: And cry “Your will?” Have you no ears? I am

speaker: ANTONY
text: Antony yet.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Take hence this jack and whip him.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: ’Tis better playing with a lion’s whelp

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Than with an old one dying.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Moon and stars!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Whip him! Were ’t twenty of the greatest tributaries

speaker: ANTONY
text: That do acknowledge Caesar, should I find them

speaker: ANTONY
text: So saucy with the hand of she here—what’s her name

speaker: ANTONY
text: Since she was Cleopatra? Whip him, fellows,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Till like a boy you see him cringe his face

speaker: ANTONY
text: And whine aloud for mercy. Take him hence.

speaker: THIDIAS
text: Mark Antony—

speaker: ANTONY
text: Tug him away. Being whipped,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Bring him again. This jack of Caesar’s shall

speaker: ANTONY
text: Bear us an errand to him.

speaker: ANTONY
text:
To
                 
                Cleopatra
                .
                 
              You were half blasted ere I knew you.Ha!

speaker: ANTONY
text: Have I my pillow left unpressed in Rome,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Forborne the getting of a lawful race,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And by a gem of women, to be abused

speaker: ANTONY
text: By one that looks on feeders?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Good my lord—

speaker: ANTONY
text: You have been a boggler ever.

speaker: ANTONY
text: But when we in our viciousness grow hard—

speaker: ANTONY
text: O, misery on ’t!—the wise gods seel our eyes,

speaker: ANTONY
text: In our own filth drop our clear judgments, make us

speaker: ANTONY
text: Adore our errors, laugh at ’s while we strut

speaker: ANTONY
text: To our confusion.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: O, is ’t come to this?

speaker: ANTONY
text: I found you as a morsel cold upon

speaker: ANTONY
text: Dead Caesar’s trencher; nay, you were a fragment

speaker: ANTONY
text: Of Gneius Pompey’s, besides what hotter hours,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Unregistered in vulgar fame, you have

speaker: ANTONY
text: Luxuriously picked out. For I am sure,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Though you can guess what temperance should be,

speaker: ANTONY
text: You know not what it is.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Wherefore is this?

speaker: ANTONY
text: To let a fellow that will take rewards

speaker: ANTONY
text: And say “God quit you!” be familiar with

speaker: ANTONY
text: My playfellow, your hand, this kingly seal

speaker: ANTONY
text: And plighter of high hearts! O, that I were

speaker: ANTONY
text: Upon the hill of Basan, to outroar

speaker: ANTONY
text: The hornèd herd! For I have savage cause,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And to proclaim it civilly were like

speaker: ANTONY
text: A haltered neck which does the hangman thank

speaker: ANTONY
text: For being yare about him.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Is he whipped?

speaker: SERVANT
text: Soundly, my lord.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Cried he? And begged he pardon?

speaker: SERVANT
text: He did ask favor.

speaker: ANTONY
text: If that thy father live, let him repent

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thou wast not made his daughter; and be thou sorry

speaker: ANTONY
text: To follow Caesar in his triumph, since

speaker: ANTONY
text: Thou hast been whipped for following him. Henceforth

speaker: ANTONY
text: The white hand of a lady fever thee;

speaker: ANTONY
text: Shake thou to look on ’t. Get thee back to Caesar.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Tell him thy entertainment. Look thou say

speaker: ANTONY
text: He makes me angry with him; for he seems

speaker: ANTONY
text: Proud and disdainful, harping on what I am,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Not what he knew I was. He makes me angry,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And at this time most easy ’tis to do ’t,

speaker: ANTONY
text: When my good stars that were my former guides

speaker: ANTONY
text: Have empty left their orbs and shot their fires

speaker: ANTONY
text: Into th’ abysm of hell. If he mislike

speaker: ANTONY
text: My speech and what is done, tell him he has

speaker: ANTONY
text: Hipparchus, my enfranchèd bondman, whom

speaker: ANTONY
text: He may at pleasure whip, or hang, or torture,

speaker: ANTONY
text: As he shall like to quit me. Urge it thou.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Hence with thy stripes, begone!

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Have you done yet?

speaker: ANTONY
text: Alack, our terrene moon is now eclipsed,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And it portends alone the fall of Antony.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I must stay his time.

speaker: ANTONY
text: To flatter Caesar, would you mingle eyes

speaker: ANTONY
text: With one that ties his points?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Not know me yet?

speaker: ANTONY
text: Coldhearted toward me?

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Ah, dear, if I be so,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: From my cold heart let heaven engender hail

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And poison it in the source, and the first stone

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Drop in my neck; as it determines, so

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Dissolve my life! The next Caesarion smite,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Till by degrees the memory of my womb,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Together with my brave Egyptians all,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: By the discandying of this pelleted storm

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Lie graveless till the flies and gnats of Nile

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Have buried them for prey!

speaker: ANTONY
text: I am satisfied.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Caesar sits down in Alexandria, where

speaker: ANTONY
text: I will oppose his fate. Our force by land

speaker: ANTONY
text: Hath nobly held; our severed navy too

speaker: ANTONY
text: Have knit again, and fleet, threatening most sealike.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Where hast thou been, my heart? Dost thou hear, lady?

speaker: ANTONY
text: If from the field I shall return once more

speaker: ANTONY
text: To kiss these lips, I will appear in blood.

speaker: ANTONY
text: I and my sword will earn our chronicle.

speaker: ANTONY
text: There’s hope in ’t yet.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: That’s my brave lord!

speaker: ANTONY
text: I will be treble-sinewed, -hearted, -breathed,

speaker: ANTONY
text: And fight maliciously; for when mine hours

speaker: ANTONY
text: Were nice and lucky, men did ransom lives

speaker: ANTONY
text: Of me for jests. But now I’ll set my teeth

speaker: ANTONY
text: And send to darkness all that stop me. Come,

speaker: ANTONY
text: Let’s have one other gaudy night. Call to me

speaker: ANTONY
text: All my sad captains. Fill our bowls once more.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Let’s mock the midnight bell.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: It is my birthday.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: I had thought t’ have held it poor. But since my lord

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Is Antony again, I will be Cleopatra.

speaker: ANTONY
text: We will yet do well.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Call all his noble captains to my lord.

speaker: ANTONY
text: Do so; we’ll speak to them, and tonight I’ll force

speaker: ANTONY
text: The wine peep through their scars.—Come on, my queen,

speaker: ANTONY
text: There’s sap in ’t yet. The next time I do fight

speaker: ANTONY
text: I’ll make Death love me, for I will contend

speaker: ANTONY
text: Even with his pestilent scythe.

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Now he’ll outstare the lightning. To be furious

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Is to be frighted out of fear, and in that mood

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: The dove will peck the estridge; and I see still

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: A diminution in our captain’s brain

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Restores his heart. When valor preys on reason,

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: It eats the sword it fights with. I will seek

speaker: ENOBARBUS
text: Some way to leave him.
